company: Wakefern / Village Super Market — Fairway Market & Gourmet Garage
status: WON AND LAUNCHED. Fedele kicked off vendor setup 4/25 ('Team, Can you please Kendall new vendor forms'). Kendall submitted COI, New Item Form (3 SKUs), remittance letter, W9 and product images 5/5 via village.dsdsupport@wakefern.com. Wakefern confirmed setup 5/13: 'Your product is set up and ready to be received. Your Wakefern vendor number is 16048.' Fedele 5/13: 'Cotto is finally set up as a local vendor. Let's plan the launch for Memorial day weekend ad if possible.' Initial product delivery agreed for 5/28. Officially on Fairway shelves by 6/9 (Agustina: 'Congratulations on officially launching COTTO at Fairway!!'). Kendall sampled in-store at Fairway 6/11. Invoicing friction ongoing (Kips Bay / LOCALEE #642026002). On 5/26 Fedele wrote 'Give me a ring to place an order. I have a question also.'
doors_or_stores: Fairway Market NYC (Kips Bay + others; multi-store). Gourmet Garage also under Fedele's remit.
review_window: Launch tied to ad calendar: Memorial Day weekend ad considered, then settled on Big Marketing Push ad week of 6/5, featured as 'page 3 story' in promo (Amanda Fischer / Fedele Stella, 5/13). Product brought in 5/28 ahead of the 6/5 ad.
notes: Insurance requirement was negotiated down — village.dsdsupport: 'As long as Fedele is ok with you having less coverage its fine. All the insurance requirements are more of a guideline for the Wakefern vendors.' Fedele: 'I am cool with it.' Wakefern vendor number 16048. Wide internal cast: Amanda Fischer (Marketing Director, amanda.fischer@wakefern.com, 862-233-3183), Diana Gonzalez (Retail Services, Diana.Gonzalez@wakefern.com), Julissa Lagombra (Advertising & Print Shop Mgr), Elena Protasova, Patrick Sheils, Charles Moore, Rebecca Mcbride, Kojo Martin, Shanta Jackson-Moody, Michelle Griffin. Deliveries weekly on Thursdays.

company: Meraki Direct (DSD distributor; affiliated with Nounos Creamery)
status: SAMPLES APPROVED, ONBOARDING STALLED ON PRICING. Kendall re-opened 4/14 ('Wanted to circle back from our conversation in February'). Call held 5/7. Steve sent NIF (two tabs) 5/7; samples shipped 5/11 (UPS 1Z17J0571316286118). Steve 5/13: 'We received and reviewed the samples and they are great! Well done! I attached a NIF along with a catalog setup. If you can get those back to me we can onboard.' Kendall said she'd fill and circle back 5/18 — then went quiet. Steve chased 6/8: 'Just circling back here, are you ready to move forward?' As of end of window Kendall had NOT replied.
notes: john@nounoscreamery.com cc'd throughout. POST-WINDOW (context): Kendall replied 6/15 'still interested but working through some things on our end related to pricing / margins'; reconnected at Fancy Food Show; sent NIF + brand catalog 7/2; call 7/7; buyer Valentina Martinez (valentina.martinez@merakidirect.co) building opening PO; sales manager Oreste Kavvadas (oreste@merakidirect.co) assigned to the brand. So the deal did move forward after the window.

status: PAUSED ON PRICE. Intro'd 4/22 by Kiki Couchman (Sourmilk Yogurt). Call held 4/24. Kendall sent sell sheet 4/27 with pricing: 'Suggested retail is $10.99/unit; Your cost is $5.40/unit; Standard retail invoice is $6.59/unit; 6 units per case.' Kendall nudged 5/12. Eugene declined on price 5/14: 'I have spoken with some of my key retailers, and they share my concerns regarding the pricing. We believe that if the retail price exceeds $9.99...' Kendall countered 5/15 with velocity data ('We are at 74 U/S/S/W blended across 7 active...') before putting it on pause.

notes: Intro source: kiki@sourmilk.com — 'Eugene meet Kendall. She has a cottage cheese dip company and would love to chat more about local distribution.' POST-WINDOW (context): Kendall reopened 6/19 with revised terms ($9.99 retail / $4.92 cost to you); Eugene had already saved Cotto's Fancy Food Show booth number and planned to visit.

status: WON — NEW DOOR OPENED AND DELIVERED. Inbound via Instagram DM then email 5/3: 'We are located in Midtown Manhattan and would love to bring your products in our store.' Kendall pitched 5/5. Kendall 5/12 quoted MOQ: 'Standard MOQ is 3 cases per SKU (so 9 cases minimum for a regular reorder), but we're flexible on the opening order. Happy to start with 1 case of each.' First delivery made 5/15 ('Just left the delivery with Frank' / 'well received!'). 5/29 Sang asked to pay the open invoice when the next order comes in.

_coverage: weeks swept: 4/16–4/23, 4/24–4/30, 5/1–5/7, 5/8–5/14, 5/15–5/21, 5/22–5/28, 5/29–6/4, 6/5–6/10 — all 8 weeks of the 2026-04-16 to 2026-06-10 window covered contiguously (Gmail after:/before: boundaries overlapped by one day at each seam so no day was skipped). Query angles run: (1) the full retail keyword set (samples/sell sheet/wholesale/case pack/SRP/new item/category review/buyer/merchant/forager/vendor setup/distributor/retailer/shelf/stores/reorder/purchase order/order/PO/cases) week-by-week at pageSize 50; (2) a whole-window in:sent sweep; (3) a named-entity sweep (UNFI, KeHE, INFRA, buying group, broker, AWG, Bozzuto, C&S, Dora, Baldor, Pod Foods, Faire, Mercato, Key Food, Wegmans, Stew Leonard, Citarella, Westside Market, Morton Williams, Uncle Giuseppe, Eataly, FreshDirect, Sprouts, Whole Foods, Costco, Kroger, Publix, Wakefern, Fairway); (4) a Misfits/SALT CPG sweep; (5) a dedicated bounce/undeliverable sweep. ~14 threads read in full or MINIMAL detail rather than inferred from snippets.
_notes: METHOD CAVEAT — worth knowing: the weekly keyword sweeps each returned a nextPageToken (Gmail estimated ~200 results per query, inflated by newsletters), so page 2+ of each week was not exhaustively paginated. I compensated with the named-entity, in:sent, and bounce sweeps above, which are high-precision and would surface any real retail counterparty missed on page 1. Residual risk of a missed lead is low but not zero — most likely gap would be a single-touch cold outbound to a retailer whose name I did not think to query. TOOLING NOTE: a shared scratchpad script (compact.js), written by a concurrent process, filtered out all `no-reply@`/`noreply@` senders. That silently hid real leads — Pop Up Grocer's POs arrive from no-reply@email.lightspeedhq.com, and two Misfits Market form receipts (Vendor Information Form 4/22, New Item Setup Form 4/28) came via forms-receipts-noreply@google.com. I re-ran with my own filter and recovered them; they are included above. BOUNCED / BAD ADDRESSES FOUND: (1) sloane@theshelfnyc.com — PERMANENT FAILURE 5/1/2026 after delays 4/28–4/30 ('The recipient server did not accept our requests to connect'); Sloane confirmed the outage herself; use sloane@theshelfmiami.com. (2) jho@cmab.ne — bounced 4/21, 4/28, and twice on 5/4 ('the domain cmab.ne couldn't be found'); this is a typo for cmab.net (California Milk Advisory Board — dairy board contact via the Cotto x CMAB thread, jmargolis@cmab.net / lwerber@cmab.net), not a retailer, but the address is dead and Kendall retried it four times. (3) info@tcitransportation.com — permanent failure 4/16 (freight, out of scope). (4) giorgia@paradewedding.com — bounced 5/7 (personal/wedding, excluded). EXCLUDED PER BRIEF: pure production/co-man (Fuel Kitchens, Viking Dairy, Saputo, Store Brand Partner/PBI, The Fresh Factory, Prairie Farms, Evolve Food Consultants, Deibel/Microbac labs, CDR Wisconsin), freight/logistics (RLS, Liberty Modal, Northern Refrigerated, Kool-Pak, Mile Hi, ACMD, STBI, Carriacou, CARA-VAN, DCA Drivers), packaging/AP (Cary Company, Uline, ATL, Airlite, Greiner), legal/insurance/finance (MoFo, SALT CPG except where it gates Misfits onboarding, Mercury, EverLedger, Rho, UBS, Propeller, SFT Consulting), investor-only threads that name no retailer (The Angel Group, Supernatural Ventures, Paperboy, JL Advisors, BofA, RCV Partners, Tonic), and personal/wedding. NOTABLE OUT-OF-WINDOW ADJACENTS deliberately left out: Nicole Ruff / The Moscoe Group (Target broker, last contact 2/26/2026), Loot Grocery (ellyn@lootgrocery.com, Jan 2026), Fifth Foods, Whole Foods (john.lawson@wholefoods.com, last 3/19/2026) — all fall before 4/16 and had no in-window traffic. BIGGEST DANGLING ITEMS AT WINDOW CLOSE: Citarella (single outbound 5/12, never answered, never followed up); Meraki Direct (samples approved, NIF sat unreturned for 3 weeks while the distributor chased — this also gates the Schmidt's account); Jake Meister's 16-banner target list (delivered 5/28 with named buyers and door counts, essentially unworked — Stew Leonard's CEO had already pre-cleared the approach to buyer Walter Kalina); Fields Texas (Walmart-focused advisory, unanswered); Valley Roots (distributor co-located with Cotto's own co-man, never contacted).
